result:
## `USE_JEMALLOC` — Memory allocator subsystem (32 sites, 13 files)

All of these gate jemalloc-specific allocator code (`je_*` calls, `mallctl`, tcache management, fragmentation hints):

| File | Lines | What it guards |
|---|---|---|
| `src/zmalloc.h` | 31, 82, 89, 135, 141 | `ZMALLOC_LIB` version string, `HAVE_DEFRAG`/`HAVE_ALLOC_WITH_USIZE` feature macros, `zmalloc_with_flags`/`zfree_no_tcache` decls |
| `src/zmalloc.c` | 65, 309, 359, 637, 693, 897 | `malloc()` macro → `je_malloc`, `zmalloc_with_flags`, `zmalloc_no_tcache`, free-stat update, page-size detection, jemalloc stats |
| `src/sds.c` | 403, 1519, 1527, 1535, 1543, 1551 | `je_nallocx`-based size-class rounding for sds allocations |
| `src/object.c` | 864, 1943 | refcount/jemalloc arena purge on object free; `MEMORY DOCTOR`/`jemalloc info` reporting |
| `src/db.c` | 1232, 1371 | forcing jemalloc to release pages back to the OS after flush/expire |
| `src/lazyfree.c` | 73 | clearing the calling thread's jemalloc tcache in the lazyfree background thread |
| `src/script.c` | 67 | passing `luaAlloc` as jemalloc-backed allocator to `lua_newstate` |
| `src/eval.c` | 21, 266, 275 | Lua state's private jemalloc tcache create/destroy (legacy EVAL engine) |
| `src/function_lua.c` | 27, 196, 206 | same private-tcache create/destroy for the FUNCTION/Lua engine |
| `src/cluster_asm.c` | 3746 | `jemalloc_purge()` call during cluster ASM (auto-shard-migration) memory cleanup |
| `src/syscheck.c` | 135 | warning text about disabling THP conflicting with jemalloc |
| `src/server.c` | 7609 | server startup/threading jemalloc-arena related setup (Linux-only) |
| `src/debug.c` | 349, 460, 1104 | `DEBUG MALLCTL` command implementation — exposes raw `je_mallctl()` to clients |

→ Firmly the **memory allocator** subsystem. Note: the 3 `debug.c` sites are physically inside the debug-command dispatcher file, but functionally they *are* jemalloc allocator control (`DEBUG MALLCTL` just forwards to `je_mallctl`) — not crash/stack-trace code, so still counted as allocator, not "crash/debug reporting".

## `HAVE_BACKTRACE` — Crash/debug reporting subsystem (11 sites, 1 file: `src/debug.c` only)

| Line | What it guards |
|---|---|
| 35 | `#include <execinfo.h>` (backtrace API availability) |
| 1260, 1381 | `logStackTrace(NULL, 1, 0)` calls from watchdog/assert-failure paths |
| 1408 | guards the `getAndSetMcontextEip` helper (reads/sets instruction pointer from a `ucontext_t`) |
| 2101 | `setupStacktracePipe()` — pipe used to capture backtraces from signal context (Linux only) |
| 2111 | `BACKTRACE_MAX_SIZE` + the actual `backtrace()`/`backtrace_symbols()` capture logic |
| 2564, 2598 | SIGSEGV/fatal-signal handler: extracting EIP and dumping code around it |
| 2752, 2766 | building the crash-log stack trace from the signal handler's `ucontext_t` |
| 2818 | additional Linux-specific crash-report detail (register/stack dump) |

→ Entirely the **crash/debug reporting** subsystem — this is Redis's fatal-signal handler / `DEBUG SEGFAULT` / watchdog stack-trace machinery in `debug.c`, gated because `execinfo.h`/`backtrace()` isn't available on all platforms (e.g. `__OpenBSD__`).

## Summary
- `USE_JEMALLOC`: 32 `#if(def)` sites across 13 files → **memory allocator** subsystem (zmalloc/sds/object/db/lazyfree/script/eval/function_lua/cluster_asm/syscheck/server, plus jemalloc-control command in debug.c).
- `HAVE_BACKTRACE`: 11 `#if(def)` sites, all in `src/debug.c` → **crash/debug reporting** subsystem (signal handlers, stack-trace capture).

Method note: `ccodegraph`'s graph models functions/globals/macros/call edges, not `#ifdef` regions, so this was answered via a scoped `grep -rnE` over `src/` for both spellings (`#ifdef X` and `#if defined(X)`), then inspected each site's surrounding code to classify subsystem. No files were modified.
